coverage.type: Central Government (Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs)
coverage.states_applicable: All Urban Areas in States/UTs (as per Census 2011 and subsequently notified towns)

who_is_it_for.short: Eligible urban households without a pucca house
detailed: Urban households in EWS, LIG and MIG categories without a pucca house anywhere in India
Slum dwellers living in inadequate housing in notified or non-notified slums
Beneficiaries identified and verified by States/UTs and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) as per PMAY-U guidelines

benefits.short: Central assistance and housing support through four verticals
details: Central assistance to States/UTs and implementing agencies through four verticals: In-situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), and Beneficiary-led Individual House construction/enhancement (BLC)
For EWS, minimum carpet area generally up to 30 square metres (with flexibility to States/UTs subject to Ministry approval)
Under CLSS, eligible beneficiaries receive interest subsidy on housing loans; subsidy processed through Central Nodal Agencies via CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Beneficiary family must not own a pucca house in any part of India in the name of any family member
Beneficiary family is defined to include husband, wife, unmarried sons and unmarried daughters
Income category (EWS/LIG/MIG) and urban status determined as per latest limits notified by MoHUA and adopted by State/UT
Ownership/co-ownership in name of adult female member promoted in guidelines

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar and identity proof of beneficiary and family members
Proof of income or self-declaration for income category
Proof of not owning a pucca house (self-declaration / property records as per State norms)
Proof of residence in urban area or slum (ration card, voter ID, electricity bill, slum certificate etc. as applicable)
Bank account details for DBT and CLSS subsidy credit

steps: Beneficiaries identified through demand surveys and applications collected by ULBs or State agencies
Online application facility provided in earlier phases on PMAY(U) portal and UMANG; States/UTs may run own portals and outreach camps
Under CLSS, beneficiaries apply for housing loans with eligible Primary Lending Institutions which upload claims to CLAP portal for subsidy processing

common_confusions.question: Is PMAY-U same as PMAY-G (rural)?
common_confusions.answer: No. PMAY-U is only for urban areas; rural households belong under PMAY-G.

common_confusions.question: Is female ownership always mandatory?
common_confusions.answer: Guidelines emphasise female ownership/co-ownership; exact documentation and relaxations are as per State/UT and MoHUA clarifications.

common_confusions.question: Is house size fixed at 30 sqm for EWS?
common_confusions.answer: 30 sq.m is a minimum benchmark for EWS; States/UTs can approve larger sizes with Ministry approval.

limitations_and_notes: Timeline and closure of new sanctions depend on central approvals and State-wise targets; citizens should confirm if new applications are being accepted locally
Actual subsidy amounts and income limits for CLSS are as per latest MoHUA and National Housing Bank / HUDCO notifications which may be periodically revised

coverage.states_applicable: All rural areas as per SECC and State/UT lists

who_is_it_for.short: Identified rural households without pucca houses
detailed: Rural households that are houseless or living in kutcha/dilapidated houses as per Socio Economic and Caste Census (SECC) and verified lists
Priority to poorest and most vulnerable sections as determined by Ministry/States

benefits.short: Financial assistance in instalments to construct pucca houses
details: Financial assistance in instalments to construct a pucca house with hygienic cooking space; unit assistance is shared between Centre and State as per funding pattern
Use of geo-tagging and IT/DBT platforms to monitor progress and transfer assistance directly into beneficiaries’ bank accounts

eligibility_criteria.condition: Household listed in PMAY-G permanent waitlist prepared using SECC data and approved by Gram Sabha and competent authorities
Beneficiary must not already have a pucca house as per scheme definition
Vulnerability and deprivation parameters applied as per PMAY-G guidelines for prioritisation

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar and identity details of head of household
Bank account details for DBT
Land ownership or allotment proof if available (State mechanisms for landless)
SECC and Gram Sabha resolution details (maintained by local authorities)

steps: Beneficiaries are selected from SECC-based lists and Gram Sabha approvals; no open online application form
Sanctions issued by block/district authorities and uploaded on PMAY-G MIS system
Beneficiaries receive instalments linked to construction stages; progress verified through field inspections and geo-tagged photographs

common_confusions.question: Can citizens self-apply online?
common_confusions.answer: Generally no; citizens must be part of approved lists and approach Gram Panchayat for clarification.

common_confusions.question: How to change beneficiary name?
common_confusions.answer: Approach local authorities with supporting documents; changes follow PMAY-G guidelines.

limitations_and_notes: Assistance amount, cost norms and unit size may differ by State/UT and are revised from time to time
Land availability for landless households handled through convergence with other schemes and State policies

coverage.type: Central Government (Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare)
coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories of India (based on land records)

detailed: Small and marginal farmer families and other eligible landholding farmer families meeting PM-KISAN guidelines
A farmer family generally defined as husband, wife and minor children who collectively own cultivable land as per land records

benefits.short: Direct income support transferred to bank accounts
details: Direct transfer of income support in equal instalments each year to eligible farmer families via DBT
Scheme operational with multiple instalments released (portal indicated 16th instalment release in Feb 2024 in provided content)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Eligibility primarily based on ownership of cultivable land recorded in State/UT land records
Certain higher economic status categories are excluded (e.g., institutional landholders, specified professionals and high-income categories) as listed on PM-KISAN portal
eKYC / Aadhaar seeding with bank accounts and land records is required for registered farmers

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar of farmer and family members
Land ownership documents or records of rights
Bank account details for DBT
Mobile number for OTP-based services

steps: Farmers can register via State/UT agriculture department offices, designated nodal officers, Common Service Centres (CSCs), or online on PM-KISAN portal subject to State adoption
eKYC completed via OTP-based method on portal or biometric eKYC at CSCs
After verification by State/UT authorities and Aadhaar/bank validation, beneficiaries are included and receive instalments

common_confusions.question: Are tenant/sharecropper farmers eligible?
common_confusions.answer: Scheme primarily for recorded land-owning farmer families; tenant/sharecropper coverage depends on State decisions based on guidelines.

common_confusions.question: Why are instalments pending?
common_confusions.answer: Often due to incomplete eKYC, Aadhaar-bank mismatch or land record issues; portal and helpline support resolution.

limitations_and_notes: Benefit amount, instalment schedule and exclusions are governed by central notifications and can be updated; farmers should check latest FAQs and guidelines on PM-KISAN portal

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)

coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories of India (empanelled hospitals nationwide)

detailed: Beneficiary families identified through SECC and State/UT approved inclusion criteria
Vulnerable rural and urban households with deprivation indicators as defined in scheme guidelines

benefits.short: Cashless hospitalisation cover for secondary and tertiary care
details: Health cover up to a specified annual amount per family (family floater) for listed procedures in empanelled hospitals
Cashless and paperless access to services at the point of care using e-cards
Coverage includes hospitalisation, diagnostics, medicines, pre- and post-hospitalisation and ICU costs (as per PMJAY package lists)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Eligibility based on SECC database and State/UT approved lists
Beneficiaries should confirm status via PM-JAY or NHA beneficiary portals

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar and other photo identity proofs as accepted by State Health Agency
Family details for beneficiary identification
PM-JAY e-card and identity proof for hospital admission and portability

steps: Beneficiaries identified from government databases and verified at designated centres (no general open online enrolment)
Check eligibility on official portals or visit empanelled hospitals / CSCs / Ayushman Mitra helpdesks for assistance
Upon verification, generate or print Ayushman e-card for cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals

common_confusions.question: Can anyone self-register online?
common_confusions.answer: AB-PMJAY primarily uses existing databases and notified inclusion criteria; open self-registration is not the standard route.

common_confusions.question: Does PM-JAY combine with State schemes?
common_confusions.answer: Many States run converged or parallel schemes; beneficiaries should confirm scheme name and applicable limits on their card and State portals.

limitations_and_notes: Benefit limits, package rates and procedures covered are specified in PM-JAY package lists and are updated periodically by the National Health Authority
Actual empanelled hospitals and services vary by State/UT; use official hospital search portals for current lists

scheme_name: Public Distribution System under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) – Ration Cards

coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories (state-specific implementation)

who_is_it_for.short: Eligible households identified under NFSA and State lists
detailed: Priority households and Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) households identified by State/UT governments as per NFSA norms
Coverage determined by State-wise ceilings and criteria

benefits.short: Subsidised foodgrains through Fair Price Shops
details: Entitlement to specified quantities of foodgrains per person per month at central issue prices as notified under NFSA
Additional State schemes may provide extra commodities or quantities at subsidised rates (varies by State/UT)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Eligibility and ration card type determined by State/UT governments consistent with NFSA

required_documents.document_name: Identity documents of head of household and family members (Aadhaar, voter ID etc.)
Proof of residence (as required by State/UT)
Income or deprivation-related documents as per State rules

steps: Apply online via State Food & Civil Supplies portals or offline at local ration offices / designated centres
Local authorities verify documents and issue ration cards; Aadhaar seeding may be used for portability where applicable

common_confusions.question: Are rules same across country?
common_confusions.answer: NFSA sets national norms but exact eligibility, documentation and grievance mechanisms are handled by each State/UT PDS department.

common_confusions.question: Is portability (One Nation One Ration Card) universal?
common_confusions.answer: Portability availability and operation depends on implementation status; check the State portal.

limitations_and_notes: For exact current rules and entitlements, households must refer to their respective State Food & Civil Supplies website

coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories of India

detailed: Individuals (rural and urban) without basic savings bank accounts
Focus on economically weaker sections and excluded households

benefits.short: Basic bank account access and financial inclusion services
details: Opening of basic savings bank deposit accounts with zero minimum balance requirements
Access to RuPay debit card, direct benefit transfer (DBT), and financial inclusion services
Portal shows over 55 crore beneficiaries and significant account balances (as of early 2026 in provided content)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Any Indian citizen not having a basic savings bank account can generally open a PMJDY account subject to KYC norms and bank policies

required_documents.document_name: Officially valid documents as per KYC norms (Aadhaar, voter ID, PAN, driving licence) or simplified KYC for small accounts
Photograph and basic personal details

steps: Approach bank branches, Bank Mitras or participating banking outlets
Accounts opened on prescribed PMJDY or integrated forms; Aadhaar seeding encouraged for DBT

common_confusions.question: Can one have multiple PMJDY accounts?
common_confusions.answer: Typically one basic savings account per person is required; customers should avoid multiple PMJDY accounts.

common_confusions.question: Will PMJDY convert to regular account?
common_confusions.answer: Banks may convert PMJDY accounts to regular savings accounts as per bank policy when limits are exceeded.

limitations_and_notes: Specific insurance, overdraft and other benefits depend on updated government and bank notifications and may change over time

coverage.type: Central Government (National Savings Institute / Ministry of Finance)
coverage.states_applicable: All India (via Post Offices and authorised banks)

detailed: Girl children who are Indian residents; parents or legal guardians can open accounts in the name of a girl child

benefits.short: Long-term savings with tax benefits for girl child
details: Account can be opened till girl attains age of 10 years
One account per girl child; family limits apply as per scheme rules
Minimum deposit ₹250 and maximum deposit ₹1.5 lakh in a financial year (as per provided content)
Deposits qualify for deduction under Section 80C and interest earned exempt under Section 10
Matures after 21 years from date of opening; provisions for premature closure in case of marriage after age 18 and partial withdrawal for higher education

eligibility_criteria.condition: Account must be opened in name of a girl child resident in India before she turns 10 years old
Only one account allowed in name of a girl child (family limits apply)

required_documents.document_name: Birth certificate of the girl child
Identity and address proof of guardian
Photograph of child and guardian

steps: Open account at designated Post Office or authorised bank with required documents
Make deposits in cash/cheque/allowed modes; account transferable across India between Post Office/banks
Follow rules for withdrawals, partial withdrawals for education and premature closure as per scheme

common_confusions.question: What is the interest rate?
common_confusions.answer: Interest rate is notified quarterly by Ministry of Finance; check official notifications or bank/post office displays.

common_confusions.question: What about NRI / non-resident status?
common_confusions.answer: Rules apply regarding continuation or closure if residency status changes; consult official scheme rules.

limitations_and_notes: Exact operational details (rates, tax rules) are notified periodically; refer to National Savings Institute / India Post for current rates

coverage.type: Central Government (implemented by Oil Marketing Companies and State agencies)
coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories as per PMUY guidelines

detailed: Adult women belonging to eligible poor household categories notified under PMUY guidelines
Targeted towards women from families identified using deprivation criteria and approved lists

benefits.short: Deposit-free LPG connection and initial consumables
details: Deposit-free LPG connection in the name of the adult woman of the household
Cash assistance for PMUY connections (specified amounts indicated on PMUY portal for 14.2 kg and 5 kg cylinders as per provided content)
First LPG refill and stove (hotplate) provided free of cost along with the deposit-free connection (supplied by Oil Marketing Companies)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Applicant must be an adult woman belonging to an eligible poor household category as per PMUY guidelines
Household should not already have an LPG connection in name of any family member

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar of applicant and family members (as required)
Proof of address and poverty/deprivation status (ration card, SECC-based list inclusion or other documents as notified)
Bank account details for receipt of subsidies where applicable

steps: Apply through LPG distributors or designated channels using prescribed forms
Oil Marketing Companies verify eligibility based on government lists and documentation and release connections
PMUY portal provides state-wise numbers and operational monitoring details

common_confusions.question: Is subsidy same as free refills?
common_confusions.answer: Initial connection assistance, free first refill and stove are distinct from continuing LPG price subsidies which depend on broader LPG subsidy policy.

common_confusions.question: What if not on eligibility lists?
common_confusions.answer: Approach local LPG distributor or district nodal officers for resolution.

limitations_and_notes: LPG refill costs after the free refill must be paid by beneficiaries; other reliefs depend on broader subsidy policies
Exact eligibility categories and assistance components governed by updated PMUY guidelines and budget announcements

scheme_name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) – Pension Schemes

coverage.type: Central Government (Ministry of Rural Development) in convergence with States/UTs
coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories (state-specific implementation and top-ups)

who_is_it_for.short: Elderly, widows and persons with disabilities from eligible poor households
detailed: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS): eligible elderly poor persons
Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS): eligible widows in specified age groups
Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS): persons with severe or multiple disabilities from eligible poor households

details: Monthly pension assistance amounts under each sub-scheme as notified by Government of India and State governments
DBT progress and beneficiary counts reported on NSAP portal (continued operation through 2024–25 in provided content)

eligibility_criteria.condition: Belong to a household identified as below the poverty line or meeting deprivation criteria defined in NSAP guidelines
Meet age and disability thresholds specified for each sub-scheme

required_documents.document_name: Age proof (for old age and widow pensions)
Death certificate of husband (for widow pensions)
Disability certificate from competent medical authority (for disability pensions)
Identity and address proof and bank account details for DBT

application_process.mode: Offline (local Social Welfare / Panchayat / Municipal offices)
steps: Submit prescribed application form to Gram Panchayat/Municipal bodies or Social Welfare offices
District/State authorities prepare and approve beneficiary lists and upload to NSAP systems
Pensions disbursed regularly through bank/post office accounts via DBT

common_confusions.question: Centre vs State pension top-ups?
common_confusions.answer: Beneficiaries may receive combined amounts from NSAP and parallel State-funded schemes; NSAP portal tracks central scheme separately.

common_confusions.question: Why are payments delayed?
common_confusions.answer: Delays often linked to bank account issues, Aadhaar seeding or verification status; resolve with local officials.

limitations_and_notes: Exact pension amounts, age and disability thresholds may differ by State/UT depending on top-up schemes; consult latest government orders

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) / Skill India

coverage.type: Central Government (Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship)
coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories (through approved training centres and partners)

who_is_it_for.short: Youth and workers seeking skill training
detailed: Unemployed youth and school/college dropouts seeking skill training
Existing workers needing skill upgradation; priority groups (women, persons with disabilities, marginalised communities) under various components

details: Short-term training and Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) through approved training centres
Skill certification under National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) and placement / self-employment support facilitated by training partners and Sector Skill Councils

eligibility_criteria.condition: Indian citizens meeting age and other criteria of the current PMKVY phase

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar and identity proof
Educational qualification certificates where required by job role
Bank account details for any applicable benefits

application_process.mode: In-person enrolment at approved training centres / online discovery
steps: Search for relevant skill courses and training centres via Skill India portal or local Skill Development offices
Enroll at approved training centres as per PMKVY / State Skill Development Mission procedures
Undergo training, assessment and certification as per NSQF-aligned job roles

common_confusions.question: Are training fees charged?
common_confusions.answer: PMKVY typically funds training costs for eligible candidates at approved centres; any charges must comply with official guidelines.

common_confusions.question: Is certification nationally recognised?
common_confusions.answer: Certificates are issued under NSQF-aligned qualifications; recognition depends on sector/employer but designed to be nationally standardised.

limitations_and_notes: PMKVY operates in distinct phases; components, priority sectors and implementation details differ by phase and may be updated

coverage.type: Central Government digital platform (multiple ministries & State schemes)
coverage.states_applicable: All States and Union Territories (scheme-specific coverage)

who_is_it_for.short: Students eligible for various scholarship schemes
detailed: School, college and university students across categories (SC/ST/OBC/minorities/persons with disabilities/economically weaker sections) depending on specific hosted schemes

benefits.short: One-stop online application and DBT for multiple scholarships
details: Online application, verification and disbursal of multiple scholarship schemes through an integrated platform
Transparency and direct benefit transfer of scholarship amounts into students’ bank accounts

eligibility_criteria.condition: Eligibility varies by individual scholarship hosted on NSP (income ceilings, marks, course and institution recognition as specified)

required_documents.document_name: Aadhaar (or other ID where permitted), bank account details and student photograph
Educational certificates, mark sheets and admission proof
Category, income and domicile certificates as applicable

steps: Register on NSP and create login
Fill scholarship application form for relevant scheme, upload required documents and submit within notified timeline
Applications pass through institute-level and State/Ministry-level verification before sanction and DBT

common_confusions.question: Can students apply to multiple scholarships?
common_confusions.answer: Follow instructions on whether multiple applications are allowed and priority rules where overlaps exist.

common_confusions.question: Difference between fresh and renewal?
common_confusions.answer: Schemes differentiate fresh and renewal applications; different forms and conditions may apply.

limitations_and_notes: Portal may experience heavy traffic or maintenance; students should monitor notifications and extended deadlines published by concerned ministries
